title: Eliminate per-session FUSE-staleness bug class by auditing every scheduled agent for FUSE-relative reads of cross-session STATE_FILE data
siblings: PROP-066 (producer-side FUSE→git+git→FUSE bidirectional sync) — complementary, NOT superseded
Tinker FND-01 fix at commit 787ee38 (added CRITICAL note + clone-or-API direction for operator-directive discovery in tinker.md) — first instance of this class fixed; PROP-071 propagates the lesson
complementary_to_prop_066: PROP-066 fixes the producer side (workspace-sync's own session FUSE gets the latest git state via Step 4c). PROP-071 fixes the consumer side (every OTHER agent reads from its own clone instead of FUSE for cross-session STATE_FILE). Both are needed; neither replaces the other.

problem_statement.summary: Every scheduled task runs in its own per-session /sessions/<id>/mnt/dome-model-review/ FUSE mount, and these mounts are independently initialized. PROP-066 Step 4c only syncs the workspace-sync session's own FUSE; the propagation does NOT reach other agents' session FUSE. Any agent that reads cross-session-mutated STATE_FILE via a FUSE-relative path can see arbitrarily stale data — minutes to days behind origin/main HEAD.

observable_bugs_today.instance: INSTANCE 1 — tinker operator-directive discovery (FIXED at commit 787ee38, 2026-05-31T11:51Z)
observable_bugs_today.shape: tinker.md Pre-flight Operator Directive Discovery read monitor/tinker/operator-directives/ via FUSE-relative path. operator-directives/ is classified append_only in build.js OWNERSHIP, and walkAppendOnly skips existing files (by design — it's append-only). So additive status-transition edits (status:pending → status:completed written by another tinker session) never reach this session's FUSE.
observable_bugs_today.evidence: Three natural experiments in past 24h: (a) tinker-2026-05-31T08-54 report — 'Pre-flight Operator-Directive Discovery picked up FUSE-stale DIRECTIVE-20260531-004 as pending; cross-check against clone revealed it completed 08:42Z'. (b) tinker-2026-05-31T09-25-prop-068-impl report — 'Pre-flight operator-directive scan returned 7 pending in FUSE; clone showed only 2'. (c) tinker-2026-05-31T09-48-fuse-staleness-cleanup report — explicitly documented the bug class. Fix: tinker.md now has a CRITICAL note mandating read-from-clone or via GitHub Contents API.

observable_bugs_today.instance: INSTANCE 2 — analyst-baby Mode 1 dispatcher tracker read (NOT FIXED)
observable_bugs_today.shape: analyst-baby.md line 84-85 reads monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json and monitor/decisions/open-issues.json via FUSE-relative paths. CLEAN_CLONE is set up at line 218 (later, for cleanup) but NOT cd'd into before the dispatcher reads.
observable_bugs_today.evidence: Operator observation 2026-05-31T~15:00Z: baby run saw 10 pending tracker items while git showed 3. No re-work performed (claimed-by-other-agent guard caught it) but signal of the bug.
observable_bugs_today.verdict: OPEN — Step 4 below recommends READ_FROM_CLONE fix

step_3_cross_session_fuse_independence_f.hypothesis_tested: Per-session FUSE mounts are independently initialized and only PROP-066 Step 4c keeps the running session's own FUSE current. Cross-session FUSE refresh is NOT a feature of the cowork backend.
step_3_cross_session_fuse_independence_f.methodology_used: Natural-experiment evidence collection. Did NOT run the suggested marker-file test because (a) the bug already manifests reliably and is documented in 3 independent tinker reports today, (b) cross-session test would require coordinating with a separate scheduled agent invocation which is not within a single tinker run, (c) sufficient evidence exists to declare the hypothesis confirmed.

evidence_collected.observation: DIRECTIVE-20260531-004 was committed status:completed to origin/main at 2026-05-31T08:40Z by commit 29df258c (session A). The 08:54Z tinker run (session B) read its FUSE and saw the directive as status:pending. The 09:25Z tinker run (session C) saw 7 pending in FUSE while git had 2. The 09:48Z run (session D) saw the still-stale FUSE.
evidence_collected.interpretation: Sessions B, C, D each had FUSE mounts that NEVER received the 08:40Z update, even ~70 minutes later. This rules out FUSE eventual-consistency models (which would converge in seconds) and confirms per-session independence.

evidence_collected.experiment: Natural experiment 2 — operator's intent vs reality on workspace-sync's reach
evidence_collected.observation: Operator's verbatim statement when filing DIRECTIVE-20260531-010: 'FUSE shouldn't have been stale. there were 2 workspace sync runs between those 2 baby runs.' This expectation was that workspace-sync's git→FUSE propagation reaches other agents' sessions.
evidence_collected.interpretation: The empirical fact is that workspace-sync's PROP-066 Step 4c runs `node build.js sync-workspace` against ${WORKSPACE} = workspace-sync's own session FUSE only. There is no mechanism by which workspace-sync can write into baby's, decider's, curmudgeon's, or any other agent's session FUSE — those mounts are filesystem-isolated.

evidence_collected.experiment: Negative experiment — workspace-sync's own session DOES see fresh git data post-PROP-066
evidence_collected.observation: Workspace-sync's own runs report that its FUSE is in-sync with origin/main after Step 4c (see workspace-sync sentinel files in monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json). Universal-pusher mode confirms it can ALSO push FUSE→git for its own session.
evidence_collected.interpretation: PROP-066 correctly handles same-session bidirectional sync. The bug class targeted by PROP-071 is strictly cross-session FUSE divergence.

step_3_cross_session_fuse_independence_f.conclusion: **HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED.** Per-session FUSE mounts are independent. No cross-session freshness mechanism exists in the cowork backend that the dome project can rely on. Each scheduled agent must take responsibility for reading STATE_FILE data from a source other than its own session FUSE — either a fresh agent-owned clone or the GitHub Contents API.
step_3_cross_session_fuse_independence_f.implication_for_fix_scope: Per-agent prompt edits. Each agent that reads cross-session-mutated STATE_FILE via FUSE-relative path needs the read redirected to its existing CLEAN_CLONE (preferred — most agents already have one) or to the GitHub Contents API (fallback — for agents without an existing clone, e.g., integrity).
step_3_cross_session_fuse_independence_f.open_question_for_operator_step_5e: Could cowork session-init be configured to pull origin/main HEAD into the new session's FUSE before agent startup? If yes, this closes the entire bug class architecturally and PROP-071's per-agent patches become unnecessary. This is out-of-scope for the dome codebase (would require cowork infrastructure changes) but operator may want to file an upstream cowork request. PROP-071's per-agent fixes would still be useful as defense-in-depth even if cowork added a session-init prefetch.

source_classes.FUSE-relative: 'monitor/foo.json' with CWD=FUSE workspace (typical scheduled-task start CWD)

file_classes.STATE_FILE: Cross-session-mutated data — open-issues, closed-issues, expansion-tracker, priority-queue, attention-inbox, predictions, wins, sections, curmudgeon/tracker.json, decider-mode, closure-ledger. MUST be fresh.
file_classes.CONFIG: Slow-changing operator-curated config — rubric-config.json, math-dense-surfaces.json, content-dense-surfaces.json. FUSE-OK (operator commits explicitly; daily cadence acceptable).
file_classes.REFERENCE: Prompts, reference/ directory, CLAUDE.md, v6-restructure-map.json. FUSE-OK (changes via decider commit; clone-or-FUSE both acceptable for read-only reference).
file_classes.PER_SESSION: Session-local state — status.json, review-state.json. FUSE-OK (workspace-owned, written by the running agent itself or by workspace-sync in same session).
file_classes.ATTENTION_INBOX_SPECIAL: Intentionally FUSE-canonical per analyst.md Mode 2b (FUSE is source of truth; clone lags). NO_FIX_NEEDED.

intent_classes.RMW: Read-Modify-Write — decision based on read informs a subsequent write (highest staleness risk)
intent_classes.decision-read: Read informs which mode/path the agent takes (no write but stale decision possible)

reads.note: FND-01 fix at commit 787ee38 added a CRITICAL note above this block saying read from clone/API. However the literal code at line 85 still shows `const dir='monitor/tinker/operator-directives'` (relative) — fix is documentation-only and relies on the LLM following the CRITICAL note. Recommend code-level fix (Step 4 below).

Mode 0 Assigned-To-Tinker Scan. Reads from FUSE. Cross-session stale risk: another tinker run could have closed assigned-tinker items but FUSE still shows them. Mitigation: tinker's per-run cap is 1 item, and the close path is via HNOTE (decider actions it). But could still drive redundant Mode 0 work on stale items.
Mode 0 scan source 2. Same risk as 124.

Backlog-Trend Computation. Stale data → misleading thresholds in the run's report but no write. Today's run hit this discrepancy: my computation read clone (I cd'd in) but a literal LLM following the prompt would read FUSE. Result would be inaccurate trend lines in queue-history.jsonl.

agents.verdict: FND-01 documentation-fix is in place but the literal code still uses FUSE-relative paths. Mode 0 + Backlog-Trend reads are not covered by the FND-01 note. Recommend code-level READ_FROM_CLONE fix across all three sites.

agents.cd_into_clone: NO — Step 0a explicitly says 'Do NOT cd into the clone — existing steps below run from whatever cwd the scheduled task started from.' All relative reads are FUSE-relative.

reads.note: Verdict tally helper; informational. Decider writes data/wins.json from CLEAN_CLONE — cross-session stale risk.
Mode 0 WORKSPACE_COUNT is INTENTIONALLY FUSE-relative because line 125 compares it to a GH-API GITHUB_COUNT. This IS the FUSE staleness check itself. NO_FIX_NEEDED.
status.json is workspace-owned, written by the running agent or workspace-sync in same session. FUSE-OK.
Mode 1 dispatcher. Reads tracker for routing decision; subsequent EXP writes + tracker updates depend on this. HIGH-risk RMW path.
PROP-029 orphan check. Could miss recently-closed issues; analyst might re-assign attention to an already-handled issue. HIGH risk because analyst.md doesn't have a defensive double-check.
Mode 1b trigger. Stale → analyst could pick up a prediction another session already wrote a verdict for.
Human Notes Rule mandates dual-write FUSE+clone, so FUSE reads SHOULD have all notes. But if dual-write is ever skipped, FUSE could miss recently-added notes. MEDIUM defense-in-depth.
Analyst.md lines 198-203 explicitly document attention-inbox.json as FUSE-canonical (operator decision 2026-04-27). NO_FIX_NEEDED.
Mode 3 trigger. Same shape as line 139.
Mode 4 trigger. Globe-fingerprint tracker — lower write velocity than expansion-tracker, but still cross-session mutable.
Mode 5 trigger. Same dual-write mitigation as line 191.
Assigned-issues check (every run). Decider closes assigned-analyst issues from a clone; analyst's FUSE read could see them as still-assigned for an arbitrary lag.

agents.internal_inconsistency_flagged: Step 0a says 'Do NOT cd into the clone' (forces FUSE-relative reads), but the analyst.md Mode 2b OBE note at line 258 says 'those are decider-canonical / clone-fresh files, so use the relative-path (clone) reads'. These directly contradict each other. Recommend resolving as part of PROP-071 implementation: either (a) flip Step 0a to allow cd-into-clone for non-Mode-2b modes, or (b) update each affected read site to use ${CLEAN_CLONE}/ prefix explicitly.
agents.verdict: 8 OPEN flags (3 HIGH, 1 MEDIUM, 2 LOW, 2 MITIGATED), 2 INTENTIONAL, 1 NO_FIX_NEEDED. Highest-priority fixes: lines 139, 165, 264, 315.

agents.cd_into_clone: NO — CLEAN_CLONE setup at line 218 is for cleanup only; not cd'd into before dispatcher reads at line 84-85.

reads.note: Mode 1 BAU dispatcher. The CANONICAL instance observed 2026-05-31T~15:00Z — baby saw 10 pending vs git's 3. RMW because baby claims items and writes EXPs based on this.
Mode 1 orphan check. Could miss recently-closed assigned-analyst issues.

agents.verdict: 2 OPEN-HIGH. Both are operator-cited as the trigger for filing this directive. Highest-priority fixes in PROP-071 scope.

agents.cd_into_clone: NO top-level cd, but most reads use ${CLEAN_CLONE}/ prefix explicitly (Step 0a + downstream).

reads.note: Explicit CLEAN_CLONE path. CORRECT.
Script execution from clone. CORRECT.

Priority 1 New WIN trigger. Analyst writes new-wins from its own FUSE; the file may not have reached the decider's FUSE in this session. Could miss new WINs that need onboarding. MEDIUM because new-wins are append-only — files won't be deleted, just delayed.
Processed-proposals ledger; written by decider itself in clone. CORRECT.
Issue proposal directory listing. Same shape as new-wins/ — append-only files written by analyst from FUSE. Could miss new proposals in this session's FUSE.
Priority 2 external-reports check uses BOTH paths FUSE-relative. The open-issues read is dangerous because decider writes open-issues from clone; FUSE-read could see stale state.
Integrity report listing from clone. CORRECT.
Rewrite proposals from clone. CORRECT.
Digest from clone. CORRECT — explicitly documented as 'must run from clone'.

Priority 4 trigger. Tracker is multi-writer (analyst + decider + baby). Decider WRITES from clone but READS from FUSE here — could decide 'NO PENDING EXPANSIONS' while clone has integration-ready entries.
Priority 5b M1 sweep. Inconsistent with line 112 which uses ${CLEAN_CLONE}/.

Priority 5b M1 stale-issue sweep. Reads stale open-issues → computes sweep candidates → writes corrective ledger entries + status changes from clone. WORST CASE: decider could 'sweep' an issue that another session has already closed, double-writing the ledger or reopening a closed item.
Same M1 sweep context. Could miss recent closures and re-process.
Another open-issues read site for downstream M2 closure processing.
Same context.
Blocked-on-curmudgeon walk. RMW → writes status changes to clone.
CLONE-relative read (CLONE is decider's own clone variable). CORRECT.
CORRECT.
CORRECT.
CORRECT.

agents.verdict: Decider is partially correct — most CRITICAL paths use ${CLEAN_CLONE}/ or CLONE+'/' explicitly. But several STATE_FILE reads are still FUSE-relative, including TWO RMW-CRITICAL paths (M1 sweep at 231, blocked-on-curmudgeon walk at 509). Highest-priority fixes.

reads.note: All subsequent relative reads are clone-relative. Curmudgeon clones fresh per-run.

agents.verdict: CORRECT BY DESIGN. Curmudgeon's Step 0 clones fresh and cd's in. Followed by all reads from clone CWD. No fix needed.

reads.note: Explicit CLEAN_CLONE prefix. CORRECT.

agents.verdict: CORRECT BY DESIGN. No fix needed.

agents.cd_into_clone: NO — no clone setup at all. Pure FUSE reads.

reads.note: Integrity inspects sections.json for V6 structure checks. Stale → could report drift that's already been fixed.
Directory listing. Could miss recently-written EXPs from another session.
Pending-digest is git-owned and generated by decider — FUSE may not have it at all in this session. Defensive read.

agents.verdict: Integrity is read-only-inspection — no STATE_FILE WRITES. But stale reads produce false-positive findings that decider then has to triage (wasted cycles) or false-negative findings (missing drift). Integrity is a Haiku agent, currently has no clone. Step 4 fix: either add CLEAN_CLONE setup mirroring analyst-baby's pattern, OR switch to GitHub Contents API for STATE_FILE reads.

agents.cd_into_clone: NO clone — social uses gh CLI + curl for GitHub API reads of the dome's external repo, not the dome-review repo's state files.

reads.note: Social's dedup-before-filing rule mentions reading open/closed-issues but doesn't have a code block. If a future social run literally implements this check via FUSE-relative path, it could re-file an already-closed issue. Currently no observable instance; flagged for awareness.

agents.verdict: Mostly clean — social's reads are against the dome's external repo via gh/curl. The only dome-review STATE_FILE touch is the dedup-mention at line 374, which is prose-only. Step 4 fix: clarify in prose that dedup checks should use clone or gh api.

agents.cd_into_clone: NO clone — poller is FUSE-resident, writes to monitor/changes/ + monitor/status.json.

reads.note: Mode 1b prediction-window trigger. Stale → poller could mark a window 'closed' on a prediction another session has already updated, or miss recently-added predictions.

agents.verdict: Poller doesn't have a clone. Same fix-pattern options as integrity: add CLEAN_CLONE OR use GH-API.

reads.note: CWD is the clone via line 78. CORRECT.

agents.verdict: CORRECT BY DESIGN (cd into CLEAN_CLONE first). Currently DISABLED pending operator decision post 2026-05-21 disaster, but pattern is correct for re-enable.

agents.cd_into_clone: YES — `cd "$CLONE"` at line 106 (CLONE is workspace-sync's own clone).

reads.description: All smart_copy reads of $WORKSPACE/... files are INTENTIONAL FUSE reads — workspace-sync's job is to push FUSE→git. FUSE is the source of truth for those reads by design.

Post-PROP-066 Step 4c invokes monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js which derives 'upstream-moved' from durable on-disk state (sentinel timestamps + origin/main HEAD) — does not depend on cross-bash-session variables. CORRECT.

agents.verdict: CORRECT BY DESIGN. Workspace-sync is the producer of git→FUSE sync; its FUSE reads are intentional. Out of PROP-071 scope per directive non_goals.

agents.cd_into_clone: YES — script runs from a fresh shallow clone per SKILL.md.

reads.description: Script reads monitor/integrity/ per-run artifacts (append-only, one-file-per-run). No staleness risk because (a) each artifact file is written once and never modified, (b) the script runs from a fresh clone.

fix_pattern_a_READ_FROM_CLONE.description: Change the read path to prefix with ${CLEAN_CLONE}/ (or CLONE/ for agents using that naming) or do a top-of-procedure `cd ${CLEAN_CLONE}` before the relevant reads. Mirror the tinker.md FND-01 fix pattern (commit 787ee38) and the curmudgeon Step 0 pattern (cd ${CLONE}).
applies_to_agents: poller (2 sites, after adding CLEAN_CLONE setup)
fix_pattern_a_READ_FROM_CLONE.operator_design_question: For analyst, the existing Step 0a explicitly says 'Do NOT cd into the clone.' Two equally-valid implementation choices: (i) flip Step 0a to allow cd, OR (ii) add ${CLEAN_CLONE}/ prefix to every STATE_FILE read site without changing CWD. Choice (ii) is more conservative but verbose. Operator should pick before PROP-071 implementation. PROP-071 itself does not prescribe one — both work.

fix_pattern_b_READ_VIA_API.description: Replace fs.readFileSync('...') with a curl/gh api call against https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/main/... or gh api /repos/.../contents/.... More HTTP calls but doesn't require a local clone.
applies_to_agents: integrity (11 sites — Haiku model, no clone today, adding one would be heavier than API)
social (1 site — already uses curl pattern for external repo, extends naturally)
fix_pattern_b_READ_VIA_API.operator_design_question: Integrity is daily-cadence Haiku — 11 small curl reads per run is cheap. Adding a CLEAN_CLONE for it is also viable. Operator preference?

fix_pattern_c_NO_FIX_NEEDED.description: Read is FUSE-relative but the class is CONFIG/REFERENCE/PER_SESSION/ATTENTION_INBOX_SPECIAL, OR the FUSE-relative-ness is intentional (workspace-sync, attention-inbox-FUSE-canonical, FUSE-vs-API staleness comparator).

snippet_examples.analyst-baby_line_84_85_AFTER_option_b_p: Insert a Pre-flight subsection above line 78 that does Step 0a's CLEAN_CLONE rebase + a literal `cd "${CLEAN_CLONE}"`, then leave the dispatcher reads unchanged.

step_5d_operator_side_observability.proposal: Add a 'cross-session-fuse-staleness' check to monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md that compares this session's FUSE STATE_FILE tail to origin/main HEAD via curl. When divergence exceeds a threshold, integrity emits a finding that decider promotes to an ISS.
step_5d_operator_side_observability.alert_thresholds: info if 1 file diverges, moderate if 2+, major if 4+ or oldest divergence > 6h
step_5d_operator_side_observability.integrity_writes_finding_to: monitor/integrity/report-<ts>.json under findings[]. Decider's Priority 2b ingests integrity findings → promotes to ISS as today.
step_5d_operator_side_observability.value: Regression canary. If a future prompt change reintroduces a FUSE-relative STATE_FILE read, integrity catches it next-day rather than after the next operator-observed bug. Cheap to implement (~30 LOC added to integrity), low FUSE-staleness check cost (4 curl reads + sha compares).

step_5e_architectural_followup_for_opera.question: Can cowork session-init be configured to pull origin/main HEAD into the new session's FUSE before agent startup?
step_5e_architectural_followup_for_opera.if_yes_implication: This would close the entire bug class architecturally. Every per-agent fix proposed by PROP-071 becomes redundant (defense-in-depth, not load-bearing). Session FUSE would be at-most-N-seconds stale (the time between session init and agent dispatch).
step_5e_architectural_followup_for_opera.if_no_implication: PROP-071's per-agent fixes are the only mitigation; we are committed to maintaining N-fix-sites in N-agent-prompts forever.
step_5e_architectural_followup_for_opera.operator_action_path_if_yes: Coordinate with Anthropic cowork team to add a `pre_session_git_pull` config flag to the scheduled-task wrapper. Once available, PROP-071's per-agent fixes can be reverted (or kept as defense-in-depth).
step_5e_architectural_followup_for_opera.operator_action_path_if_no: Proceed with PROP-071's per-agent fixes as the only mitigation. Add the Step 5d observability check to catch future regressions.

estimated_implementation_effort.phase_1_critical_priority: Fix analyst-baby (2 sites), analyst (8 sites), decider RMW-CRITICAL (4 sites: 231, 232, 509, 510). ~30-45 min Opus. Highest blast-radius items.
estimated_implementation_effort.phase_2_decider_remaining: Fix decider remaining 4 sites (205, 222, 424, 425, 511, 519). ~15 min Opus.
estimated_implementation_effort.phase_3_low_priority: Fix tinker (4 sites — extends FND-01 fix), poller (2 sites + add CLEAN_CLONE setup), integrity (11 sites — operator picks API vs CLEAN_CLONE; if API, ~45 min; if CLEAN_CLONE, ~25 min). ~70-90 min total.
estimated_implementation_effort.phase_4_observability: Add structure-integrity.md fuse-staleness canary per Step 5d. ~30 min.

interaction_with_other_props.prop_066: PROP-066 (producer-side bidirectional sync) is COMPLEMENTARY to PROP-071. Both are needed. PROP-066 ensures workspace-sync's session has correct FUSE; PROP-071 ensures every other session reads from a known-fresh source.
interaction_with_other_props.prop_065: PROP-065 (git-append-only .jsonl files) is the inverse problem — preventing FUSE→git regression. PROP-071 is git→FUSE regression. Both protect cross-session integrity.
interaction_with_other_props.prop_060: PROP-060 (Mode 0 assigned-to-tinker scan) is a CONSUMER of stale data — Mode 0 itself uses FUSE-relative open-issues read at tinker.md:124. PROP-071 fixes that read site, making PROP-060 reliable cross-session.

rollback_plan.trigger: PROP-071 fixes break a previously-working agent (e.g., decider can't find CLEAN_CLONE; falls into FUSE fallback and misbehaves).
rollback_plan.mechanism: Each phase fixes a small set of read sites with clearly-scoped diffs. Operator can revert by single-commit revert per phase. The fix-pattern is mechanical — wrap relative path in ${CLEAN_CLONE}/ — so reverting is unambiguous.
rollback_plan.safety: All fixes use existing CLEAN_CLONE/CLONE infrastructure that was already set up by Step 0a / Pre-flight blocks. No new infrastructure is introduced; only existing infrastructure is consistently used.

out_of_scope_per_directive: Implementation of fixes (author-only per directive non_goal #1)
Re-fixing tinker.md FND-01 — already done at commit 787ee38 (directive non_goal #2)
System-level cross-session FUSE sync mechanism (directive non_goal #4 — needs cowork infra changes)
Superseding or amending PROP-066 (directive non_goal #5 — PROP-071 is complementary)
expected_followup_directive: After operator reviews this audit, file a DIRECTIVE-2026MMDD-NNN with target_mode set to whatever agent does the per-agent prompt edits (likely tinker via Mode 4, OR direct operator-side edits). The followup directive should pick fix-pattern A vs B for the design-question sites (analyst CD vs prefix, integrity CLEAN_CLONE vs API) before any edits land. Operator may want to split implementation into 2-3 directives by phase priority.
